info.title: DNSimple API
info.description: [DNSimple](https://dnsimple.com) provides DNS hosting and domain registration that is simple and friendly. We provide an extensive API and an easy-to-use web interface so you can get your domain registered and set up with a minimal amount of effort.

servers.description: DNSimple Sandbox API used for testing

get.description: Lists the accounts the current authenticated entity has access to.

200.description: Depending on how you are authenticated you will see all the accounts you have access to or the account you used to authenticate.

get.description: Retrieves the details about the current authenticated entity used to access the API.

200.description: Successful response with user or account.

post.summary: Exchange authorization code for access token
post.description:
Exchanges an OAuth 2.0 authorization code for an access token.
Used in the authorization code grant flow.

400.description: Invalid or expired code, invalid client credentials, or redirect_uri/state mismatch.

get.description: Lists the billing charges for the account.
Lists the domains in the account.

post.description:
Creates a domain and the corresponding zone into the account.

When creating a domain using Solo or Teams subscription, the DNS services
for the zone will be automatically enabled and this will be charged on your
following subscription renewal invoices.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing domain.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a domain from the account.

get.description: Research a domain name for availability and registration status information.
Gets the DNSSEC status for an existing domain.

post.description:
Enables DNSSEC for the domain.

It will enable signing of the zone. If the domain is registered with DNSimple, it will also add the DS record to the corresponding registry.

delete.description:
Disables DNSSEC for the domain.

It will disable signing of the zone. If the domain is registered with DNSimple, it will also remove the DS record at the registry corresponding to the disabled DNSSEC signing.

get.description: Lists the DS records for the domain.

post.description: Adds a DS record to the domain.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing DS record.

delete.description: Removes a DS record from the domain.

get.description: Lists email forwards for the domain.

post.description: Creates a new email forward for the domain.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing email forward.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an email forward.

post.description: Initiates a pust of a domain to another DNSimple account.

get.description: List pending pushes for the target account.

200.description: Successfully retrieved pushes for the account.

post.description: Accepts a push to the target account.

delete.description: Rejects a push to the target account.

get.description: Lists the certificates for a domain.

200.description: Successfully retrieved certificates for a domain.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing certificate.

200.description: Successfully retrieved a certificate for a domain.

get.description: Gets the PEM-encoded certificate, along with the root certificate and intermediate chain.
Gets the PEM-encoded certificate private key.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the private key for an issued certificate.

post.description: Orders a [Let's Encrypt](https://dnsimple.com/letsencrypt) certificate with DNSimple.

201.description: Successfully created the certificate purchase order.

post.description: Issues a [Let's Encrypt](https://dnsimple.com/letsencrypt) certificate ordered with DNSimple.

parameters.name: purchaseId
parameters.description: The certificate purchase order id received by `purchaseLetsencryptCertificate`.

202.description: Successfully requested the issuance of the certificate.

post.description: Renews a [Let's Encrypt](https://dnsimple.com/letsencrypt) certificate ordered with DNSimple.

201.description: Successfully created the certificate renewal order.

post.description: Issues a [Let's Encrypt](https://dnsimple.com/letsencrypt) certificate renewal ordered with DNSimple.

parameters.name: renewalId
parameters.description: The certificate renewal order id received by `purchaseRenewalLetsencryptCertificate`.

202.description: Successfully requested the issuance of the certificate renewal.

get.description: Lists TLDs supported for registration or transfer.
Retrieves the details of a TLD.

Lists a TLD extended attributes.

Some TLDs require extended attributes when registering or transferring a domain. This API interface provides information on the extended attributes for any particular TLD. Extended attributes are extra TLD-specific attributes, required by the TLD registry to collect extra information about the registrant or legal agreements.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the extended attributes for the TLD.

get.description: Checks a domain name for availability.

200.description: The check was successfully executed. Check the result data to determine if it is available for registration. If the domain is premium, please check the premium price before trying to register, renew, transfer.

get.description: Retrieve domain prices.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the domain pricing data

post.description:
Registers a domain name.

Your account must be active for this command to complete successfully. You will be automatically charged the registration fee upon successful registration, so please be careful with this command.

When registering a domain using Solo or Teams subscription, the DNS services
for the zone will be automatically enabled and this will be charged on your
following subscription renewal invoices.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing domain registration.

post.description:
Transfers a domain name from another registrar.

Your account must be active for this command to complete successfully. You will be automatically charged the 1-year transfer fee upon successful transfer, so please be careful with this command. The transfer may take anywhere from a few minutes up to 7 days.

When transfering a domain using Solo or Teams subscription, the DNS services
for the zone will be automatically enabled and this will be charged on your
following subscription renewal invoices.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing domain transfer.

delete.description: Cancels an in progress domain transfer.

202.description: Domain transfer cancellation accepted and queued for processing.

post.description:
Explicitly renews a domain, if the registry supports this function.

Your account must be active for this command to complete successfully. You will be automatically charged the renewal fee upon successful renewal, so please be careful with this command.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing domain renewal.

post.description:
Explicitly restores an domain that can not be renewed anymore, if the registry supports this function.

Your account must be active for this command to complete successfully. You will be automatically charged the restore fee upon successful restore, so please be careful with this command.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing domain restore.

post.description:
Prepares a domain for transferring out.

This will unlock a domain and send the authorization code to the domain's administrative contact.

get.description: Lists the name servers for the domain.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the domain name servers.
Successfully updated the domain name servers.

put.description: Delegate a domain to vanity name servers.

200.description: Successfully delegated to vanity name servers.

delete.description: De-delegate a domain from vanity name servers.

get.description: Gets the transfer lock status for a domain.

post.description: Locks the domain to prevent unauthorized transfers.

delete.description: Unlocks the domain to allow domain transfers.

put.description: Enables auto renewal for the domain.

delete.description: Disables auto renewal for the domain.

put.description:
Enables the WHOIS privacy for the domain.

Note that if the WHOIS privacy is not purchased for the domain, enabling WHOIS privacy will cause the service to be purchased for a period of 1 year. If WHOIS privacy was previously purchased and disabled, then calling this will enable the WHOIS privacy.

201.description: Successfully purchased and enabled whois privacy.

delete.description: Disables the WHOIS privacy for the domain.

get.description: List the primary servers in the account.

post.description: Creates a primary server into the account.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing primary server.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a primary server.

put.summary: Link a primary server to a secondary zone
put.description: Link the primary server to a secondary zone.

put.summary: Unlink a primary server from a secondary zone
put.description: Unlink the primary server from a secondary zone.

post.description:
Creates a secondary zone into the account.

When creating a secondary zone using Solo or Teams subscription, the DNS
services for the zone will be automatically enabled and this will be charged
on your following subscription renewal invoices.

get.description: Lists the zones in the account.
Retrieves the details of an existing zone.
Download the zonefile for an existing zone.
Checks if a zone is fully distributed to all our name servers across the globe.

504.description: The server failed to perform the check.

200.description: Successfully updated the zone name servers.

get.description: Lists the records for a zone.

post.description: Creates a new zone record.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing zone record.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a zone record.

post.summary: Batch create, update, and/or delete records in a zone
post.description: Performs multiple record operations (create, update and/or delete) for a zone in a single request for atomicity and efficiency.

200.description: Successfully processed batched zone record changes.

400.description: Invalid batch change zone records request error

get.description: Checks if a zone record is fully distributed to all our name servers across the globe.

504.description: The server failed to perform the check.

put.description:
Activate DNS services for the zone.

Under Solo and Teams plans, active zones are charged when renewing your subscription to
DNSimple

200.description: Successful activation of DNS services on the zone.

delete.description:
Deactivates DNS services for the zone.

Under Solo and Teams plans, active zones are charged when renewing your subscription to
DNSimple

200.description: Successfully deactivate DNS services on the zone.

get.description: List contacts in the account.

post.description: Creates a contact.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing contact.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a contact from the account.

get.description: List registrant changes in the account.

post.description: Start registrant change.

202.description: The contact change has started and pending completion.

post.description: Retrieves the requirements of a registrant change.

200.description: Successfully returned the registrant change requirements.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing registrant change.

delete.description: Cancel an ongoing registrant change from the account.

get.description: List all available one-click services.
Retrieves the details of a one-click service.
List services applied to a domain.

200.description: Successfully retrieved list of applied services.

post.description: Applies a service to a domain.

delete.description: Unapplies a service from a domain.

get.description: Lists the templates in the account.

post.description: Creates a template.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing template.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a template.

get.description: Lists the records for a template.

200.description: Successfully retrieved the template records list.

post.description: Creates a new template record.

get.description: Retrieves the details of an existing template record.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a template record.

post.description: Applies a template to a domain.

put.description:
Enables Vanity Name Servers for the domain.

This method sets up the appropriate A and AAAA records for the domain to provide vanity name servers, but it does not change the delegation for the domain. To change the delegation for domains to vanity name servers use the endpoints to Delegate to Vanity Name Servers or Dedelegate from Vanity Name Servers.

200.description: Successfully enabled vanity name servers on the domain.

delete.description:
Disables Vanity Name Servers for the domain.

This method removes the A and AAAA records required for the domain to provide vanity name servers, but it does not change the delegation for the domain. To change the delegation for domains to vanity name servers use the endpoints to Delegate to Vanity Name Servers or Dedelegate from Vanity Name Servers.

get.description: List the webhooks in the account.

post.description: Registers a webhook endpoint.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a registered webhook.

delete.description: De-registers a webhook endpoint.

get.description: Queries and returns DNS Analytics data available for the provided query parameters. This API is currently in Public Beta. During the Public Beta period, changes may occur at any time.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Only include results after the given date. Format is ISO8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). It can be a max of 31 days away from the end_date. If omitted, it defaults to 31 days ago.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Only include results before the given date. Format is ISO8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). It can be a max of 31 days away from the start_date. If omitted, it defaults to 1 day ago.

parameters.name: groupings
parameters.description: How to group the results of the account's DNS analytics. No groupings will be applied by default unless specified. Multiple groupings can be provided, separated by a comma. Applying groupings to the query will add columns to the response payload.

organization_name.description: The company name. If the organization_name is specified, then you must also include job_title.

job_title.description: The contact's job title. Required if the organization_name is specified.

NullableDate.description: A nullable date-time value. The value can be null, when present the value is formatted according to the ISO 8601 specification.

NullableDateTime.description: A nullable date-time value. The value can be null, when present the value is formatted according to the ISO 8601 specification.

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

invitation_sent_at.description: A date-time value formatted as ISO 8601.

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

BatchChangeZoneRecordsResponse.description: Response from batch change of zone records

BatchChangeZoneRecordsInvalidRequestErro.description: Client request error for batch change of zone records

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

expires_at.description: A date-time value formatted as ISO 8601.

expires_on.description: A date value formatted as ISO 8601.

reference.description: The reference number of the invoice.

product_id.description: The ID of the product that was charged. Null when the product type is "manual".

product_reference.description:
A unique or representative reference. For example, the domain name that was charged.
Or the ID of the subscription or the order ID of the product. Null when the product
type is "manual"

TemplateRecordType.description: Supported DNS record types for templates.

tld.description: The registerable suffix. In most cases the suffix matches a TLD, but there are some cases where the suffix is a subzone of the TLD (e.g. com.au).

whois_privacy.description: true if the suffix allows WHOIS privacy as a separate component. Some registries may provide WHOIS privacy at registry level by limiting the visibility of certain attributes in the WHOIS response.

auto_renew_only.description: true if the suffix requires auto-renewal and can't be renewed manually

idn.description: true if the suffix supports Internationalized Domain Names (IDN)

registration_enabled.description: true if DNSimple supports registrations for this TLD

renewal_enabled.description: true if DNSimple supports renewals for this TLD

transfer_enabled.description: true if DNSimple supports inbound transfers for this TLD

name_server_min.description: minimum number of name servers required for this TLD

name_server_max.description: maximum number of name servers allowed for this TLD

trustee_service_enabled.description: true if trustee service is enabled by default for this TLD

trustee_service_required.description: true if trustee is required for this TLD

TldType.description: "TLD types are: * 1 - gTLD (global TLD) * 2 - ccTLD (country-code TLD) * 3 - newTLD (new global TLD)"

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

request_identifier.description: The request_identifier attribute is a UUID that provides a way to identify this request. You may use this UUID to ensure a webhook is processed once and only once by keeping a persistent history of the identifiers sent and never handling a webhook which was already processed.

expires_on.description: A date value formatted as ISO 8601.

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

reverse.description: Returns true for a reverse zone, false for a forward zone.

secondary.description: Returns true for a secondary zone, false for a primary zone.

last_transferred_at.description: Returns the date the zone was last transferred. Used if it is a secondary zone.

active.description: Returns true if DNS services are active for the zone. If false, DNS services are disabled and DNS records will not be resolved.

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

example.zone:
$ORIGIN example.com.
$TTL 1h
example.com. 3600 IN SOA ns1.dnsimple.com. admin.dnsimple.com. 1453132552 86400 7200 604800 300
example.com. 3600 IN NS ns1.dnsimple.com.
example.com. 3600 IN NS ns2.dnsimple.com.
example.com. 3600 IN NS ns3.dnsimple.com.
example.com. 3600 IN NS ns4.dnsimple.com.

distributed.description: Returns true if the zone is fully distributed, false if not.

id.description: The unique identifier for the record.

zone_id.description: The unique identifier of the zone this record belongs to

parent_id.description: If present represents the zone record this record relates to. The parent record is the master record, when the parent is updated or deleted the related record is also updated or deleted. An example of child record is the TXT descriptive record created for an ALIAS record.

name.description: The name of the record (e.g., "www" for www.example.com).

content.description: The content/value of the record (e.g., IP address for A records, hostname for CNAME records).

ttl.description: The Time To Live (TTL) value for the entry, in seconds.

priority.description: The priority value for MX and SRV records. Lower values have higher priority.

regions.description: The regions where this record is active. If empty, the record is active in all regions.

system_record.description: Returns true if a record is a system generated record and thus is not modifiable.

created_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was created, formatted as ISO 8601.

updated_at.description: A date-time value, representing when the entry was last updated, formatted as ISO 8601.

ZoneRecord.description: A single DNS record in a zone.

ZoneRecordType.description: The type of DNS record. Supported DNS record types are listed below. Note that some record types may only be available on specific plans.

registrant_id.description: The ID of an existing contact in your account.

whois_privacy.description: "Set to true will attempt to purchase/enable the whois privacy as part of the registration. An extra cost may apply. Default: false."

auto_renew.description: "Set to true to enable the auto-renewal of the domain. Default: false."

trustee.description: "Set to true will attempt to purchase/enable trustee service as part of the registration. An extra cost may apply. Default: false."

premium_price.description: Required as confirmation of the price, only if the domain is premium.

linked_provider.description: The nickname of a linked provider to register the domain through. If left blank, registration will go through DNSimple.

premium_price.description: Required as confirmation of the price, only if the domain is premium.
Required as confirmation of the price, only if the domain is premium.

registrant_id.description: The ID of an existing contact in your account.

auth_code.description: Required for TLDS that require authorization-based transfer (the vast majority of TLDs).

whois_privacy.description: "Set to true will attempt to purchase/enable the whois privacy as part of the transfer. An extra cost may apply. Default: false."

auto_renew.description: "Set to true to enable the auto-renewal of the domain. Default: false."

trustee.description: "Set to true to use a trustee service for the transfer. An extra cost may apply. Default: false."

premium_price.description: Required as confirmation of the price, only if the domain is premium.

alias_name.description: The receiving email recipient, the name part only without the domain. The domain is automatically appended.

destination_email.description: The email recipient the messages are delivered to.

new_account_identifier.description: The account identifier of the target DNSimple account

ttl.description: The Time To Live (TTL) value for the entry, in seconds.
The Time To Live (TTL) value for the entry, in seconds.

WebhookPayloadData.description: The data attribute contains any data for the object or objects related to the event. Each object in the data object will be keyed on an object type name.

WebhookPayloadAccount.description: The account is an object describing which account the event occurred in. It is a lightweight representation of the account and includes the id, a unique identifier and a display text representation.

WebhookPayloadActor.description: "The actor is an object describing the entity that triggered the event. This may be a user if the event was triggered due to activity via the UI or API, or it may be a system entity if the activity occurred as part of an asynchronous process. The actor object has three attributes: id, which is a unique identifier for the actor, entity which describes what type the actor is, and pretty, which is a printable representation of the actor for use in display."

id.description: ID of the deleted zone record

ttl.description: The Time To Live (TTL) value for the entry, in seconds.

id.description: ID of the zone record to update

ttl.description: The Time To Live (TTL) value for the entry, in seconds.

id.description: ID of the zone record to delete

DomainRegisterRequestExtendedAttributes.description: Required for TLDs that require extended attributes.

DomainTransferRequestExtendedAttributes.description: Required for TLDs that require extended attributes.

index.description: Index of the failed create operation
Index of the failed update operation
Index of the failed delete operation

redirect_uri.description: Must match the redirect_uri from the authorization request (used for validation only).

state.description: Must match the state from the authorization request.

error.description: Error code per OAuth 2.0 spec (e.g. invalid_request).

auto_renew.description: Enable the auto-renewal of the certificate

signature_algorithm.description: Optional string to determine the signature algorithm to be used. Either `ECDSA` or `RSA`

auto_renew.description: Enable the auto-renewal of the certificate

signature_algorithm.description: Optional string to determine the signature algorithm to be used. Either `ECDSA` or `RSA`

ExamplePayload.description: Placeholder schema for path example refs

anyOf.description: A date value formatted as ISO 8601.

DomainRegisterRequestExtendedAttributesP.description: Required for TLDs that require extended attributes.

DomainTransferRequestExtendedAttributesP.description: Required for TLDs that require extended attributes.

sort.description: Sorting applied to the returned data.

groupings.description: Grouping applied to the returned data.

per_page.description: The page size used to generate the data.

TldDnssecInterfaceType.description: type of data interface required for DNSSEC

WebhookPayloadName.description: The name attribute contains a string representing the name of event that occurred. It will always have an object and action separated by a period. It may optionally include a state for objects that go through state changes, for example `domain.registration:started`.

WebhookPayloadApiVersion.description: The api_version is the API version used to serialize the data in the payload. For version 2 of the API, this is the string v2. For example, you can expect a domain object to be serialized using the same domain attributes described in the corresponding API page.

400.description: A required parameter is missing or the request is invalid.

phone: is probably not a phone number

402.description: Your account is not subscribed or not in good standing.

value.message: This action cannot be completed because you have purchase invoices that need to be paid for: 12345-1010. Please retry the payment following these instructions https://support.dnsimple.com/articles/account-invoice-history/#retrying.

412.description: A precondition failed while trying to access a resource.

429.description: You exceeded the allowed number of requests per hour and your request has temporarily been throttled.

412-feature.description: Feature is not available on your plan.

Template.name: template
Template.description: The template id or short name

FilterName.name: name
FilterName.description: Only include results with a name field exactly matching the given string

FilterNameLike.name: name_like
FilterNameLike.description: Only include results with a name field containing the given string

FilterRegistrantId.name: registrant_id
FilterRegistrantId.description: Only include results with the registrant_id field matching the given value

FilterStartDate.name: start_date
FilterStartDate.description: Only include results after the given date. Format is ISO8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

FilterEndDate.name: end_date
FilterEndDate.description: Only include results before the given date. Format is ISO8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

FilterType.name: type
FilterType.description: Only include results with a type field exactly matching the given string

FilterRegistrantChangeState.name: state
FilterRegistrantChangeState.description: Only include results with a state field exactly matching the given string

FilterDomainId.name: domain_id
FilterDomainId.description: Only include results with a domain_id field exactly matching the given string

FilterContactId.name: contact_id
FilterContactId.description: Only include results with a contact_id field exactly matching the given string

PaginationPage.name: page
PaginationPage.description: The page of the results requested. Defaults to 1.

PaginationPerPage.name: per_page
PaginationPerPage.description: The amount of rows to return in each page. The default is usually 100. There are endpoints that override this default to accommodate specific use cases.

SortCertificates.name: sort
SortCertificates.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id.

SortCharges.name: sort
SortCharges.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by invoiced ascending.

SortContacts.name: sort
SortContacts.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id ascending.

SortDNSAnalytics.name: sort
SortDNSAnalytics.description: Sort results. Default sorting policy is by ascending date, then ascending zone_name.

SortDomains.name: sort
SortDomains.description: Sort results. Default sorting is ascending by name.

SortDelegationSignerRecords.name: sort
SortDelegationSignerRecords.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id.

SortEmailForwards.name: sort
SortEmailForwards.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id.

SortPrimaryServers.name: sort
SortPrimaryServers.description: Sort results. Default sorting is ascending by id.

SortRegistrantChanges.name: sort
SortRegistrantChanges.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id.

SortServices.name: sort
SortServices.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id ascending.

SortTemplates.name: sort
SortTemplates.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id ascending.

SortTemplateRecords.name: sort
SortTemplateRecords.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by id ascending.

SortTLDs.name: sort
SortTLDs.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by tld ascending.

SortWebhooks.name: sort
SortWebhooks.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by name ascending.

SortZones.name: sort
SortZones.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by name ascending.

SortZoneRecords.name: sort
SortZoneRecords.description: Sort results. Default sorting is by name ascending.

BatchChangeZoneRecords.description: Batch change zone records input attributes

DomainNameServers.description: A collection of domain name servers

PushAccept.description: Attributes required to accept a push

ServiceApply.description: Optional hash of settings for some specific services, corresponding to the service fields. For instance, Heroku requires a settings[app] setting.

TemplateRecordCreateOrUpdate.description: Attributes for creating a template record

VanityNameServers.description: A collection of vanity name servers

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: The maximum number of requests you can perform per hour.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The time at which the current rate limit window in Unix time format.
